fixes_applied: Added castShadow attribute to all three mesh objects
kimi_reasoning: Image 1 (PORTED) displays a completely black viewport with no 3D content rendered, showing only the UI sidebar with example listings. Image 2 (REFERENCE) shows the correctly rendered 'Simple procedurally-generated shapes' demo with multiple colorful 3D shapes (smiley faces, hearts, circles, rectangles, capsules, organic shapes) arranged in a radial pattern on a light gray background, each with dotted trail outlines. The ported version has failed to render the 3D scene entirely, resulting in a black canvas where the shapes should be. The background color, lighting, objects, and scene composition are completely different between the two images.
kimi_key_differences: Main viewport content: PORTED shows solid black empty canvas, REFERENCE shows colorful 3D procedural shapes
Background color: PORTED has black background, REFERENCE has light gray/white background

Scene objects: PORTED has zero 3D objects visible, REFERENCE has 8+ distinct shapes (magenta smileys, green circles, black twisted shape, blue rectangles, orange California shape, red heart, brown circle, teal capsule, gray rounded shape)
Visual effects: PORTED has no dotted trail outlines, REFERENCE has dotted line trails behind each shape
Text/UI: PORTED shows sidebar navigation with '@woby/three' header, REFERENCE shows title 'Simple procedurally-generated shapes' and FPS counter
Lighting: PORTED has no visible lighting (black screen), REFERENCE has fully lit scene with shadows and ambient light
